author: arnetheduck
content:
Based on a few discussions, I've written down the semantics of top-up sync as it works today and potentially in gloas: https://hackmd.io/@arnetheduck/HJx05fJCWg 

This is a method of quickly and deterministically syncing up the EL based on CL data when both are reasonably close to head (such as after a restart, a short period of downtime or just after a snap/checkpoint sync of both)

The minimal requirement for gloas is that we continue serving range requests for execution payloads, ie that we serve https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/v1.7.0-alpha.5/specs/gloas/p2p-interface.md#executionpayloadenvelopesbyrange-v1

Clients can ignore this feature for as long as ELs support syncing via devp2p (ELs driven by a CL that supports top-up sync can remove support for devp2p block sync entirely assuming they have a way of syncing a state within the CL block retention period)
